gaps: No existing test case covers the 'Apps' settings section for Focus 2 devices.
No test case verifies the Device settings page content/structure after pairing a Focus 2 headset.
No test case validates that the 'Apps' section appears under Device settings for any paired device.
All existing test cases focus on onboarding, EULA, splash screen, and location permissions — none cover post-pairing device settings UI.

generated_testcases.title: Verify 'Apps' settings section appears for paired Focus 2 device
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete the onboarding flow.
2. Grant all required permissions (location, Bluetooth, etc.).
3. Pair a Focus 2 headset with the app.
4. Once paired, navigate to the Device settings page for the Focus 2 device.
5. Observe the list of settings sections displayed on the Device settings page.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section is visible and listed among other settings sections on the Device settings page for the paired Focus 2 device.

generated_testcases.title: Verify Device settings page structure for Focus 2 includes all expected sections
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete the onboarding flow.
2. Grant all required permissions.
3. Pair a Focus 2 headset with the app.
4. Navigate to the Device settings page for the paired Focus 2 device.
5. Scroll through the entire settings page and document all visible settings sections.
generated_testcases.expected: The Device settings page for Focus 2 displays the following sections: 'Apps', 'General', 'Audio', 'Voice', 'Display', 'Controls', 'Gestures', 'Developer Options' (or equivalent expected sections per product spec). The 'Apps' section is present and accessible.

generated_testcases.title: Verify 'Apps' section is accessible and opens correctly from Focus 2 Device settings
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete the onboarding flow.
2. Grant all required permissions.
3. Pair a Focus 2 headset with the app.
4. Navigate to the Device settings page for the paired Focus 2 device.
5. Tap on the 'Apps' settings section.
generated_testcases.expected: Tapping 'Apps' opens the Apps settings page, which displays a list of installed or available apps for the Focus 2 device. The page loads without errors and shows relevant app management options.

generated_testcases.title: Verify 'Apps' section persists after re-entering Device settings for Focus 2
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete the onboarding flow.
2. Grant all required permissions.
3. Pair a Focus 2 headset with the app.
4. Navigate to the Device settings page and confirm the 'Apps' section is visible.
5. Navigate away from the Device settings page (e.g., go back to the main screen).
6. Re-enter the Device settings page for the Focus 2 device.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section is still visible on the Device settings page after navigating away and returning. The section is consistently displayed.

generated_testcases.title: Verify 'Apps' section is absent for non-Focus 2 devices (specificity check)
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete the onboarding flow.
2. Grant all required permissions.
3. Pair a non-Focus 2 device (e.g., Focus 1 or another supported headset model).
4. Navigate to the Device settings page for the paired non-Focus 2 device.
5. Observe the list of settings sections displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section is NOT present on the Device settings page for non-Focus 2 devices, confirming the section is specific to Focus 2.

generated_testcases.title: Verify 'Apps' section appears after re-pairing Focus 2 device
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete the onboarding flow.
2. Grant all required permissions.
3. Pair a Focus 2 headset with the app.
4. Navigate to the Device settings page and confirm the 'Apps' section is visible.
5. Unpair/forget the Focus 2 device.
6. Pair the same Focus 2 headset again.
7. Navigate to the Device settings page for the re-paired Focus 2 device.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' settings section is visible on the Device settings page after re-pairing the Focus 2 device, confirming the section appears consistently across pairing sessions.
